subject.name: AI Engineering
subject.description: Production-focused tracks for building, evaluating, and operating AI systems.

topic.name: RAG Systems
topic.description: Retrieval-augmented generation systems for grounded AI applications.

course.title: RAG Systems Essentials
course.description: Build a foundation in retrieval, chunking, embeddings, ranking, and grounded generation for RAG applications.
learnObjectives: Explain the parts of a RAG pipeline

modules.name: Retrieval Pipeline
modules.description: Understand how documents move from source content to retrieved context.

lessons.name: RAG Architecture Overview
lessons.description: Map the major components of a retrieval-augmented generation system.

content.overview: RAG combines document retrieval with generation so answers can be grounded in external knowledge.
content.body:
A basic RAG system ingests documents, chunks them, embeds them, stores vectors, retrieves relevant chunks, and passes those chunks to an LLM.

Each stage affects answer quality. Weak chunking, poor metadata, or noisy retrieval can cause the model to answer with incomplete context.

lessons.name: Chunking and Metadata
lessons.description: Learn why chunk size and metadata shape retrieval quality.

content.overview: Chunking controls what information can be retrieved together; metadata helps filter and explain results.
content.body:
Chunks should be large enough to preserve meaning and small enough to retrieve precisely.

Metadata such as source, section, date, and document type can improve filtering and make citations easier.
